title: Improve GST Calculation Precision

requirements.text: Improve GST calculation precision in the backend Motoko actor to eliminate rounding errors. Use high-precision integer arithmetic (paise-level) throughout all GST computation steps — including CGST, SGST, and IGST — ensuring that intermediate values are never truncated or rounded prematurely. Final amounts should only be rounded at the very last step before storage.

quotes: ok gst needs for precise I guess

acceptanceCriteria: CGST + SGST amounts always sum exactly to the total GST amount without off-by-one paise errors
IGST amount matches the expected GST rate applied to the taxable value with no rounding discrepancy
Intermediate GST computation values are not truncated mid-calculation
Invoice totals stored in the backend match the amounts displayed on the invoice to the last paise

requirements.text: Improve GST calculation precision in the frontend gstUtils.ts utility to exactly mirror the updated backend logic. Ensure all per-line-item and full-invoice GST summary calculations use integer paise arithmetic throughout, rounding only at the final display step. The frontend computed totals must match backend-stored values exactly.

quotes: ok gst needs for precise I guess

acceptanceCriteria: Frontend GST summary totals (CGST, SGST, IGST, taxable amount, grand total) match backend-stored values exactly for all invoices
No floating-point arithmetic is used in intermediate GST calculation steps in gstUtils.ts
InvoicesTab.tsx and InvoiceDetails.tsx display GST values consistent with backend data

constraints: Do not change the invoice or transaction data schema in a way that would corrupt existing stored records
GST calculation logic must remain consistent between frontend (gstUtils.ts) and backend (main.mo)
nonGoals: Changing the GST rate structure or tax categories
Adding new invoice fields or UI layout changes
Modifying authentication or user profile features
